utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final plan: it includes health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget — so 5 items appear. Next, verify if each is fully specified with required details: - health_coverage: needs physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan gives physical_health €45/month, mental_health €30/month, and a total. This is fully specified. - property_coverage: needs home/contents insurance with costs. The plan provides 'Enhanced Expedition Photographer Coverage' with €40/month, but it does not explicitly state home/contents insurance; it's tailored to photography equipment. This does not satisfy 'home/contents insurance' as required. - travel_coverage: needs travel insurance with costs. The plan includes a description and €20/month cost; this is fully specified. - additional_services: needs any enhancement services with costs. The plan says 'None included' — while acceptable as an explicit statement, it does not list services with costs, but 'None' is a valid complete specification (no services, hence no costs). - total_budget: needs complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. The plan provides only a monthly total (€135/month) and no annual breakdown, so it is not fully specified. Therefore, fully specified items are: health_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services. That is 3 out of 5.

Therefore, I can map the two SecureLife Basic Plan items and the TravelCo Basic Plan. The property coverage item cannot be mapped as its name doesn't match any ground-truth option.
